claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're asking about.

Let me start with: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. My son Alex is 13 years old and lives with me here in England.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Alex is 13 years old and lives with you in England - that's helpful.

Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, do you provide for Alex's care and upbringing, such as providing food, clothing, and a home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for Alex. I provide everything he needs - food, clothing, a home, all of that. He lives with me and I take care of him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living situation:

**Do you normally live in the UK?** Or are you subject to immigration control that would prevent you from claiming public funds?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK. I'm not subject to any immigration control - I have the right to claim public funds.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?** 

(Full-time non-advanced education typically means school, or college courses up to A-levels or equivalent. This question becomes particularly relevant for young people aged 16-19.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He's only 13, so he's just in regular secondary school - nothing like A-levels or college yet. He's still doing his normal school years.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Regular secondary school at age 13 is perfectly fine - that's standard education for his age.

Let me ask: **Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period?** And if so, has this been for more than 12 weeks (or is it expected to be)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex is at home with me - he's not in hospital or in any kind of care. He's just living a normal life at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for all that information. Let me ask one final question:

**Is there anyone else who might also be responsible for Alex - for example, another parent or guardian who also provides care for him?** If so, do they live with you or does Alex spend time living with them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else. It's just me - I'm the only one responsible for Alex. He lives with me full-time and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility criteria:

✓ **Alex is under 16** - He's 13 years old, which is within the age limit for Child Benefit (under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training)

✓ **You're responsible for Alex** - You provide for his care and upbringing, including food, clothing, and a home

✓ **Alex lives with you** - He lives with you full-time in England

✓ **You meet the residence requirements** - You normally live in the UK and are not subject to immigration control that would prevent you from claiming public funds

✓ **No competing circumstances** - Alex is not in hospital or local authority care for an extended period, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

**Next steps:** You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. You'll need to claim within 3 months of Alex's birth or the date you became responsible for him to get the full amount - otherwise your claim will usually only be backdated by up to 3 months.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, which is under the age limit for Child Benefit (under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training). The claimant is responsible for Alex, providing for his care and upbringing including food, clothing, and a home. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant normally lives in the UK and is not subject to immigration control that would prevent claiming public funds. Alex is not in hospital or local authority care for an extended period. There is no other person claiming Child Benefit for Alex or who might have priority. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is the sole person responsible for their 13-year-old son Alex, who lives with them in the UK. The claimant meets all the residence and immigration requirements, and Alex meets the age criteria. There are no circumstances that would prevent eligibility such as extended hospital/care stays or competing claims. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
